summary: I was born and raised in Karen State, which is part of Burma. Growing up there, I started learning to read and write at a very young age, using whatever materials I could find, like a calendar. My early education and experiences in Karen State shaped how I value time and literacy.

Burma, also known as Myanmar, is where I come from, specifically from Karen State. My grandfather taught me important lessons about time and calendars there, which helped me learn to read and write. Life in Burma influenced my understanding of time management, which I carried with me when I moved abroad.

I arrived in the United States in December 2015, bringing with me a calendar filled with notes and family appointments. Living here has allowed me to continue my education and adapt my skills, especially in managing time and planning. The United States has become my new home where I am raising my children and pursuing my studies.

Currently, I live in St. Paul, Minnesota, where I am continuing my education and raising my family. St. Paul is where I attend classes at the Hubbs Center, and it has become a place of learning and growth for me. This city is important because it supports my journey as an immigrant and a student.

I am a student at the Hubbs Center in St. Paul, Minnesota, where I am continuing my education after arriving in the United States. The Hubbs Center provides me with opportunities to learn and improve my skills, helping me adjust to life here. It has become an important part of my journey as an immigrant and learner.
